mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near a cyclical trough with no upward drift signals continued accommodative monetary policy, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and supporting a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as investors reprice growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 in triggered subsample over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a low or falling federal funds rate jointly signal robust consumer demand financed under cheap credit conditions, producing a compounding positive effect on S&P 500 returns over the next quarter that neither series alone can explain.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with resilient consumer spending
failure_condition: Joint-trigger subsample shows Rank IC not exceeding single-factor RSAFS-only model by more than 0.02, indicating no genuine interaction effect, over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A steady month-over-month increase in industrial production reflects expanding real economic capacity and corporate output, which supports near-term equity risk appetite and produces modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 across two consecutive 2-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, decelerating but still-positive nonfarm payroll growth signals labor market maturation without imminent recession, allowing S&P 500 returns to remain positive over a quarterly horizon as markets price a soft-landing scenario; the same deceleration in an early-recession regime instead predicts negative returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth positive but below trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: Sign of the PAYEMS-deceleration to forward-return relationship flips (becomes negative) in late-cycle regime samples with statistical significance p < 0.05, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts exceeding 10% over a 2-month period signals an incipient contraction in interest-rate-sensitive demand, which historically leads broader economic slowdowns and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of HOUST decline are followed by positive median 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC in triggered subsample is below 0.03 across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in CPI inflation above trend occurring while the federal funds rate remains near zero signals a policy lag in which real rates fall further negative, triggering a short-term speculative equity rally over the next trading week as investors anticipate delayed but eventual tightening.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary regime with rising inflation
failure_condition: Joint-trigger subsample fails to show statistically distinct return distribution from unconditional 5-day return distribution (Kolmogorov-Smirnov p > 0.10) across any evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a positive but below-potential real GDP level relative to prior peak signals ongoing slack that supports continued monetary and fiscal support, producing above-average S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as markets price continued stimulus; this relationship reverses once GDP fully recovers to trend.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 output-gap proxy and forward 60-day returns falls below 0.03 in post-shock regime samples, or the effect direction reverses with statistical significance during confirmed recovery periods.
